mappings.rationale: Directly covers kinship systems, marriage customs, descent rules, and cultural institutions across societies.
Covers the major social structures and institutions including class systems, economic organization, and socialization.
Directly addresses legal aspects of marriage, divorce, legitimacy, and family relationships covered in subsection A.
Covers formal and informal education and socialization, directly relevant to subsection D.1.
Covers recreation, sports, and games as cultural institutions, directly relevant to subsection D.6.
Covers sexual behaviour customs and marriage norms across cultures, relevant to subsection A.2.
Covers ritual as a major cultural institution common to all societies but varying in expression.
Covers musical expression as a cultural institution that differs between cultures, relevant to subsection D.4.
Covers capitalist economic systems as a major type of economic organization in developed nations.
Covers economic systems including production, division of labour, exchange of goods, and property rights.
Covers class systems and status-based organization, directly relevant to subsection B.2.
Covers how social identity is constructed through kinship, group membership, and cultural institutions.
Directly addresses marriage customs and practices that vary across cultures, relevant to subsection A.2.
Covers division of labour and role differentiation as elements of economic systems across cultures.
Covers how cultural institutions differ between groups within diverse societies.
Covers myth and folklore as elements common to all cultures but differing in expression, relevant to subsection D.2.
Covers mythological traditions as cultural institutions that differ in expression between cultures.
Covers modern institutional approaches to property transmission and economic organization in developed nations.
Covers the challenge to slavery and serfdom as forms of social organization, relevant to subsection B.2.
Covers slavery as a system of social organization characterized by unfree status, relevant to subsection B.2.
Covers challenges to kinship structures, marriage customs, and gender-based role differentiation across cultures.
Covers group dynamics and social structures including in-group/out-group dynamics relevant to subsection B.1.
Covers historical changes in kinship, marriage, descent, and gendered social structures across cultures.
Covers socialization and enculturation of children as a cultural institution, relevant to subsection D.1.
Covers Confucian kinship systems, filial piety, and family as a center for transmitting cultural values.
